summary: Assembly test `tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs` failed because FileCheck verification found that expected strings were not present in the generated WebAssembly assembly output.
root_cause: FileCheck assertions at lines 38 and 62 of tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs expected specific strings in the generated assembly, but these strings were not found in the actual wasm_exceptions.s output. The generated assembly contains `call may_panic` instructions, but the test's CHECK directives did not match the expected patterns.

evidence.quote: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: FileCheck tool reported that the expected pattern in the assembly test was not found in the generated code

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:38:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: Explicit failure at line 38 of the test file with CHECK directive mismatch

evidence.quote: /checkout/tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs:62:12: error: CHECK: expected string not found in input
evidence.reason: Second failure at line 62 of the test file with CHECK directive mismatch
FileCheck tool explicitly failed to verify the assembly matches the expected patterns

evidence.quote: test result: FAILED. 550 passed; 1 failed; 103 ignored
evidence.reason: Only the assembly test failed; all other tests in the suite passed, indicating this is an isolated test assertion failure

suggested_fix: Review the expected CHECK patterns in tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs (lines 38 and 62) and update them to match the actual generated wasm_exceptions.s assembly output, or investigate whether recent changes to WebAssembly exception handling code generation have altered the assembly structure in an unintended way.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
